quiz_items.question: A laser show uses a mirror ball to create effects. When is a scanning safeguard required on the mirror ball?
options: Always required on all mirror balls
Depends on beam peak power and minimum scan pattern size at the mirror ball
Only if the mirror ball rotates
quiz_items.explanation: According to the guide, whether a scanning safeguard is needed on a mirror ball depends on factors such as the beam peak power and the minimum size of the scan pattern at the mirror ball.
quiz_items.regulatory_reference: Appendix B - Scanning Safeguards on Mirror Balls

quiz_items.question: Under what conditions may beam stops be omitted without additional protection? (Select all that apply)
options: Employees educated by laser safety officer
Control measures implemented per recognized safety standards
quiz_items.explanation: Beam stops may be omitted if: (a) areas occupied only by employees, (b) employees educated by LSO about hazards, and (c) control measures posted per recognized safety standards (like ANSI Z136.1).
